stories.headline: House Energy & Commerce holds hearing today on AI data centers and ratepayer protection
stories.summary: Energy and Commerce convenes at 10:15 a.m. to weigh how to build out AI data center load without shifting costs onto residential and small-business ratepayers — central to the PJM cost-allocation fight.

stories.headline: Two more offshore wind projects scrapped as developers cite cost pressure
stories.summary: Utility Dive reports two additional U.S. offshore wind projects have been canceled, deepening the pipeline collapse that has reshaped Murphy-era NJ procurement targets and BPU planning assumptions.

stories.headline: House Republicans introduce bill to extend renewables tax credits
stories.summary: A House GOP bill would extend wind, solar and storage tax credits beyond their current sunsets, a notable break from the broader party line and a potential boost for co-op generation and RUS-backed projects.

stories.headline: PJM market monitor warns of another gas plant deal raising market-power concerns
stories.summary: PJM's independent market monitor flagged a pending gas plant transaction as a potential market-power risk, the latest signal that capacity-market consolidation is drawing regulatory scrutiny inside the RTO.

stories.headline: House GOP rule vote on FISA, farm bill and ICE funding looks headed for failure
stories.summary: Speaker Johnson goes to the floor today on a multi-bill rule covering FISA Section 702 (expires Thursday), the farm bill, ICE/Border Patrol funding and E15 ethanol, with Boebert and Mace publicly opposed and Senate GOP openly frustrated.

stories.headline: Hegseth and Caine face first public Hill grilling on Iran war and $1.5T FY27 defense budget
stories.summary: Defense Secretary Hegseth and Joint Chiefs Chair Gen. Dan Caine testify at 10 a.m. before House Armed Services as the Iran war nears 60 days, with Reps. Crow and Deluzio (Pa.) ready to press on cost, end-state and Strait of Hormuz blockade plans.

stories.headline: Senate Banking votes on Kevin Warsh as next Fed chair after Tillis-led Powell standoff
stories.summary: Banking Committee meets at 10 a.m. to advance Warsh's nomination on a likely party-line vote; Sen. Thom Tillis cleared the path after DOJ ended its Powell probe, with floor confirmation expected by mid-May and a May 15 start date.

stories.headline: Burgum and Zeldin face Senate budget hearings on Interior and EPA
stories.summary: Interior Secretary Doug Burgum testifies before Senate ENR at 9:30 a.m. and EPA Administrator Lee Zeldin appears before EPW at 2:30 p.m., the first major airing of the administration's FY27 energy and environmental priorities.

stories.headline: Florida Republicans advance gerrymandered congressional map that could net GOP four seats
stories.summary: DeSantis's redistricting plan cleared the Florida House yesterday despite some GOP defections over legality concerns, with the state Senate expected to vote Thursday on a map that could swing as many as four U.S. House seats to Republicans.

stories.headline: Emerson poll gives Democrats 10-point generic ballot lead heading into midterms
stories.summary: A new Emerson College survey of likely voters has Democrats up 50-40 over Republicans on the generic congressional ballot, with the spread driven by gains among Hispanic voters, women and independents — a flashing warning for PA delegation Republicans.

stories.headline: Rep. Rob Bresnahan picks up Boilermakers Local 13 endorsement
stories.summary: PA-08's Bresnahan announced the endorsement of Boilermakers Local Lodge No. 13, a building-trades nod in a swing district that overlaps Claverack and Sullivan REC service territory — useful for the labor-aligned energy-build messaging.

stories.headline: PA Supreme Court orders public release of 2020 cast vote records
stories.summary: The Pennsylvania Supreme Court ruled Tuesday that ballot-level cast vote record spreadsheets are public records, granting access to a researcher hired by the Trump administration and reopening a five-year-old election-data fight.

stories.headline: PA House advances bill banning whites-only housing, adding LGBTQ anti-discrimination protections
stories.summary: The PA House moved a measure pairing a ban on segregationist housing developments with new LGBTQ protections under the state's anti-discrimination law, the latest attempt to expand the Human Relations Act after years of stalled standalone bills.

stories.headline: Penguins face Game 6 elimination in Philadelphia, down 3-2 in first round
stories.summary: Pittsburgh hits Xfinity Mobile Arena at 7:30 p.m. on TNT facing elimination in the Battle of Pennsylvania, riding a 3-2 Game 5 win Monday after rallying from a 3-1 series deficit against the Flyers.

stories.headline: Cardinals pound Pirates 11-7 at PNC; Cruz and O'Hearn homer in loss
stories.summary: Oneil Cruz hit his ninth homer leading off and Ryan O'Hearn added a two-run shot, but Pittsburgh's pitching staff coughed up 11 runs as Kyle Leahy went 5⅓ for the win and the Pirates dropped their third straight (16-15).

stories.headline: Steelers place UFA tender on Aaron Rodgers; OTAs open May 18
stories.summary: Pittsburgh placed the unrestricted free-agent tender on the 42-year-old QB, preserving exclusive negotiating rights past July 22 and a comp-pick option if he signs elsewhere; voluntary OTAs begin May 18, mandatory minicamp June 2-4.
